activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I'll call you when I arrive at the hotel.
activities.explanation: No hay error: tras «when» usamos presente simple («arrive») aunque la llegada sea futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: We'll eat as soon as Dad will get home.
activities.explanation: Hay un error: después de «as soon as» debe decir «Dad gets home», sin «will».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: Please lock the door before you leave.
activities.explanation: No hay error: «before you leave» usa presente simple para una acción futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I won't start cooking until you will be here.
activities.explanation: Tras «until» usamos presente: la forma adecuada es «until you are here».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: We'll have coffee after the meeting finishes.
activities.explanation: La oración es correcta: «finishes» aparece en presente después de «after».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: Once the rain stops, we'll continue the match.
activities.explanation: No hay error: «once» introduce «stops» en presente y la principal lleva «will».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: Once the rain will stop, we'll continue the match.
activities.explanation: La cláusula con «once» no admite «will» en este uso: debe ser «Once the rain stops».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I'll send the form back as soon as I complete it.
activities.explanation: La secuencia es correcta: «complete» está en presente tras «as soon as».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: Before the guests will arrive, we'll put the drinks in the fridge.
activities.explanation: Después de «before» corresponde presente simple: «Before the guests arrive».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: We'll stay inside until the storm passes.
activities.explanation: Es correcta: «until» va seguido del presente «passes» para marcar el límite futuro.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: When the new term begins, I will join the evening class.
activities.explanation: La cláusula temporal usa correctamente «begins» y la consecuencia futura usa «will join».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I'll keep the receipt in case we need to return the headphones.
activities.explanation: No hay error: «in case» se combina con presente para una posible necesidad futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I'll keep the receipt in case we will need to return the headphones.
activities.explanation: Con «in case» no usamos normalmente «will» para esta posibilidad: debe decir «in case we need».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: After you have read the instructions, the system will ask you to confirm.
activities.explanation: Es correcta: el presente perfecto «have read» puede enfatizar que la lectura estará terminada antes del siguiente paso.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: We'll wait here until the technician has checked the lift.
activities.explanation: El presente perfecto tras «until» es válido porque destaca la finalización de la comprobación.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: As soon as the payment will have arrived, we will dispatch your order.
activities.explanation: La subordinada debe usar presente perfecto: «As soon as the payment has arrived».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I'll decide which route to take when I see the traffic report.
activities.explanation: La decisión es futura, pero la cláusula temporal usa correctamente el presente «see».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: When will you see the traffic report?
activities.explanation: No hay error: aquí «when» introduce una pregunta directa, no una cláusula temporal subordinada, y admite «will».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I don't know when the replacement part will arrive.
activities.explanation: Es correcta: «when» introduce una pregunta indirecta sobre el momento, no una condición temporal para otra acción.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I don't know when will the replacement part arrive.
activities.explanation: La pregunta indirecta mantiene orden afirmativo: «when the replacement part will arrive».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: By the time the audience enters, the musicians will be ready.
activities.explanation: «By the time» lleva presente «enters» y la situación posterior se expresa con «will be».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: By the time the audience will enter, the musicians will be ready.
activities.explanation: En la cláusula con «by the time» debe usarse presente: «the audience enters».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: The moment the results are available, the university will publish them online.
activities.explanation: La expresión temporal «the moment» se combina correctamente con presente «are».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: I'll back up the files before I install the update.
activities.explanation: La subordinada con «before» usa presente «install» y la principal expresa la decisión futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the sentence contains an error in its future time clause.
activities.prompt: We will not know the exact cost until the engineer completes the inspection.
activities.explanation: La estructura es correcta: «until» introduce el presente «completes» y la principal usa «will not know».
